recommendation:
Peter,

I'm 17, starting Princeton this fall, and I built Aurelius (runaurelius.com). The real problem in AI infrastructure isn't power—it's that schedulers optimize the next placement, but nobody plans the fleet's trajectory over time. In a simulated replay of ~1.5M public production requests, Aurelius cut GPU-hours by 84%. If that holds up, worth a 10-minute read on the memo?

Beckett
label: best-supported candidate under the current assumptions; no reliable distinction between finalists (uncalibrated evaluator — real outreach outcomes, not more simulation, would sharpen this)
